build_sdk_options.signature: def build_sdk_options(project_root: Path, worktree: Path | None, skill_name: str, skill_args: dict, run_id: str) -> ClaudeAgentOptions
build_sdk_options.description: Centralized SDK ClaudeAgentOptions constructor. Sets setting_sources=['project'], cwd=project_root, additional_directories=[worktree] when provided, per-skill max_turns, per-skill allowed_tools/disallowed_tools, can_use_tool callback, no-op PreToolUse hook, env={} (scrub credentials; rely on proxy), never bypassPermissions. Single source of truth for every skill-route SDK invocation; unit-tested that setting_sources is always ['project'].

AgentSessionManager.signature: class AgentSessionManager: async def start_run(project_id, skill_name, args) -> AgentRun; async def get_run(run_id) -> AgentRun; async def send_input(run_id, text) -> None; async def interrupt(run_id) -> None; async def attach_events(run_id, since_seq: int = 0) -> AsyncIterator[AgentEvent]
AgentSessionManager.description: Long-lived lifespan-managed service owning all ClaudeSDKClient instances. Per-project asyncio.Semaphore (cap 3, configurable) for concurrency. Per-set SQLite unique constraint (project_id, set_id, status='running') for semantic mutex -- second run rejected with 409. Crash recovery marks in-flight runs 'interrupted' on startup; no SDK session resume across restarts. Orphan-process sweeper runs at startup, scanning PIDs tracked in agent_runs.pid column.

AgentSession.signature: class AgentSession: run_id: str; pid: int | None; pump_task: asyncio.Task; event_bus: EventBus; async def __aenter__(); async def __aexit__(); async def run() -> None
AgentSession.description: Wraps a single ClaudeSDKClient + asyncio pump task. Async-context-managed so subprocess is always cleaned up. Emits events via EventBus with monotonic seq numbers. asyncio.wait_for-wrapped with per-skill timeout to prevent hangs.

EventBus.signature: class EventBus: async def publish(run_id, event: AgentEvent) -> None; async def subscribe(run_id, since_seq: int = 0) -> AsyncIterator[AgentEvent]
EventBus.description: Per-run asyncio pub/sub with in-memory ring buffer (last 1000 events). Backing store is agent_events SQLite table for durable replay across reconnects and backend restarts.

can_use_tool_hook.signature: async def can_use_tool(tool_name: str, tool_args: dict, context: ToolPermissionContext) -> ToolPermissionDecision
can_use_tool_hook.description: Primary tool gate. Destructive-pattern regex (git push --force, rm -rf /, branch -D, env*, cat .env*) -> deny. Allowlisted per-skill tools -> allow. Unknown/ambiguous -> route to browser via EventBus ('permission_req' event) and await answer from POST /api/agents/runs/{id}/answer with timeout -> 409 on stale prompt_id. Paired with no-op PreToolUse hook because the SDK silently short-circuits can_use_tool without one (undocumented quirk).

disallowed_tools_registry.description: Unconditional firewall regex list for destructive Bash/Write/Edit patterns. Never overridable by any skill. Applied before allowlist check.

agent_run_model.signature: class AgentRun(SQLModel, table=True): id: str (uuid); project_id: str; set_id: str | None; skill_name: str; skill_args: str (json); status: Literal['pending','running','waiting','interrupted','failed','completed']; pid: int | None; started_at: datetime; ended_at: datetime | None; total_cost_usd: float; max_turns: int; turn_count: int; error_code: str | None; error_detail: str | None
agent_run_model.description: SQLite row per run. Unique constraint: (project_id, set_id) WHERE status='running' for per-set mutex. Canonical run identity; referenced by agent_event.run_id FK, agent_prompts.run_id FK, kanban_card.agent_run_id FK, chat_message.agent_run_id FK.

agent_event_model.signature: class AgentEvent(SQLModel, table=True): id: int (auto); run_id: str (FK); seq: int; ts: datetime; kind: Literal[...]; payload: str (json)
agent_event_model.description: Durable event log per run. Unique (run_id, seq). Supports ?since=N replay on SSE reconnect. Batched writes ~1/s to avoid SQLite write contention.

sse_event_schema.signature: kind in {assistant_text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result, ask_user, permission_req, status, run_complete}; payload shape per kind is stable typed contract
sse_event_schema.description: Typed SSE event names and payload shapes. Every consumer (web-tool-bridge, agents-chats-tabs, kanban-autopilot) depends on this contract. Client reconnect uses ?since=<seq> query param to replay from ring buffer + SQLite backfill.

run_dispatch_http.signature: POST /api/agents/runs {project_id, skill_name, skill_args} -> 200 {run_id} in <200ms; GET /api/agents/runs/{id} -> AgentRun; GET /api/agents/runs/{id}/events (SSE); POST /api/agents/runs/{id}/input {text}; POST /api/agents/runs/{id}/interrupt
run_dispatch_http.description: Task-queue dispatch shape. POST /runs returns <200ms with run_id; actual run is asyncio.Task owned by session manager. Never synchronous. No BackgroundTasks. Matches user's CLAUDE.md polling preference.

per_skill_permission_policy.description: Per-skill policy config. plan-set/execute-set: acceptEdits + scoped disallow. merge/cleanup: default. Interactive skills: default. Never bypassPermissions. Unit-tested that bypassPermissions never appears in any policy entry.

create_sdk_mcp_server_integration.signature: def register_mcp_tools(session: AgentSession, tools: list[Tool]) -> None
create_sdk_mcp_server_integration.description: Integration point for downstream sets to register in-process @tool functions. Used by web-tool-bridge (ask_user tools) and kanban-autopilot (kanban_tools) to expose MCP tools to the agent without a separate process.

run_id_correlation.signature: contextvars.ContextVar[str] named 'run_id' read by logger filter; threaded into SDK env (ClaudeAgentOptions.env['RAPID_RUN_ID']); present in every agent_event row; echoed in every .planning/ write the spawned skill performs
run_id_correlation.description: Single run_id threaded through: stdout logs, SQLite agent_events/agent_runs/agent_prompts/chat_message rows, SSE event payloads, spawned-skill environment, and .planning/ artifact commit trailers. Schema decision is load-bearing for UI capabilities for next 2 years.

rapid_run_mode_env.description: Env var gate passed to spawned skills. Skill prose branches on this to call either the SDK-only custom MCP tools (when sdk) or retain CLI AskUserQuestion behavior (when cli or unset). Enables additive-only CLI parity: no skill is forked.

cost_budget_tracking.signature: class RunBudget: max_turns: int; daily_cap_usd: float (default 10.0); async def record_turn(cost_usd) -> None; async def check() -> BudgetStatus
cost_budget_tracking.description: Per-run max_turns (from per-skill policy); per-project daily cap enforced across all runs. Halts run with 'budget_exceeded' status when hit. Surfaces 'budget remaining' via dashboard endpoint.

error_taxonomy.signature: class SdkError(Exception); class RunError(Exception); class StateError(Exception); class ToolError(Exception); class UserError(Exception)
error_taxonomy.description: Canonical error hierarchy. Every downstream set raises one of these; API error responses map error_code 1:1. Enables UI error taxonomy and BLOCKED-resume skeleton.

sub_200ms_dispatch.description: POST /api/agents/runs must return {run_id} in under 200ms; actual run executes asynchronously on an asyncio.Task. Never block the request thread on SDK startup.

setting_sources_project.description: Every ClaudeAgentOptions built by build_sdk_options() has setting_sources=['project'] so .claude/skills/*, CLAUDE.md, and slash commands are loaded. Unit test asserts this invariant on every code path.

no_bypass_permissions.description: bypassPermissions is never set on any ClaudeAgentOptions anywhere in the codebase. Unit test greps source. Propagates to subagents silently and nullifies the safety model.

credential_env_scrubbed.description: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and related credentials never appear in the spawned SDK subprocess env. Options.env is set to {} (plus RAPID_RUN_MODE + RAPID_RUN_ID only); credentials flow via proxy pattern (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL) or SDK's OAuth config, never inherited.

per_set_run_mutex.description: Starting a second run against the same (project_id, set_id) while one is 'running' fails with HTTP 409. SQLite unique partial index enforces semantically; handler returns error before dispatch.

concurrency_semaphore.description: At most 3 concurrent SDK runs per project (configurable) via asyncio.Semaphore. Prevents SDK >=0.1.59's 11+ concurrent query() warning and SQLite write contention.

destructive_commands_firewalled.description: git push --force, rm -rf /, branch -D, env*, cat .env*, and related destructive Bash patterns are always denied regardless of skill policy. can_use_tool checks DESTRUCTIVE_PATTERNS before any allowlist.

run_id_on_every_log_line.description: All log records emitted during a run include the run_id via contextvars. Every SQLite insert, every SSE event, every .planning/ artifact write carries the same run_id for correlation.

orphan_subprocess_reaping.description: On backend startup, scan agent_runs WHERE status IN ('running','waiting') and reconcile against live PIDs; mark orphans as 'interrupted'. Prevents cost-burning zombies.

cwd_is_project_root.description: SDK session cwd is always the project root. Worktree paths are exposed via additional_directories so spawned skills can cd into worktrees for git ops but session transcripts survive worktree cleanup.
